mandarin_name: Lazy Pasta 慵懶義式廚房文山政大店
address: No. 161, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan 116
opening_hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM
review_1: I ordered a vegetarian portion. It went beyond what I had expected. The meal is not too heavy with vegetables, the noodles are stir-fried with mushrooms and zucchini to create an irresistible aroma.
review_2: Bacon spaghetti with garlic oils tastes so good and not that heavy to eat. It is also the recommended menu. So many people came here for dinner, need to wait first. The price seems up-middle average, a little bit more expensive than what I expected. The waitress was really kind and friendly.
review_3: Delicious pasta. The price just raised for a bit but still reasonable. 1 and a half hour time limit is perfectly enough for a friend catch-up. You can also get free bread and green tea for joining membership.
review_4: This place has its purpose and it serves it well. To offer cheap pasta in a clean environment. I also highly value that they are open the whole day. The staff is also very attentive and friendly.
review_5: A nice pasta place in the Chengta campus area with efficient service, decent food and clean toilet.  The tables are small and crammed together like most places in the area but still comfortable for enjoying a meal. Expect noise when it's crowded. The aisle and spacing are not designed for wheelchair users.

mandarin_name: FEED ZONE CAFE 運動早午餐
address: 1F, No.189, Xingguang Rd, Section 1, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan, 116
opening_hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 10:30 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:30 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 10:30 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 10:30 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 8:30 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 8:30 AM – 5:00 PM
review_1: Great place for a coffee or to grab a delicious quick bite. Amazing bikes and accessories as well, they even sell 'A Cyclist's Guide to Taiwan' 😊
review_2: Incredibly tasty food—I totally did not expect this. Friendly owner and even some pretty delicious drinks. Really enjoyed the soicey chicken dish and the cinnamon bun.
review_3:
A must-stop for cyclists near Taipei Zoo!
This spot is a hidden treasure for bikers, perfect for a post-ride coffee break for a much-needed carb and protein refill. Whether you’ve tackled one of the challenging bike routes or just enjoyed a scenic ride, this café has exactly what you need to recharge. The location is unbeatable, right next to Taipei Zoo and surrounded by a variety of biking paths, from easy to adventurous. The shop itself is modern, stylish, and clearly designed with cyclists in mind. The owner has created a space that’s both trendy and functional.

The coffee is excellent, and their food options are thoughtfully crafted for riders, balanced with carbs and protein to help you refuel properly. They also carry a great selection of cycling accessories, including clip-in shoes, jerseys, and more. It’s also a social hub, perfect for meeting fellow riders, sharing stories, and taking a well-earned break. This is definitely a place I’ll keep coming back to.
review_4: Awesome! Derailed review later. Recommended!
review_5: Went there to grab a quick coffee and I tried the Cherry Ice coffee. It was amazing ! They offers a wide nice coffee, latte and things like that. Food also looked excellent. The setting is funny, you are surrounded by biking gears and staff was super friendly. They also offer biking gear and organise bike trip on Wednesday ! You should come and see

mandarin_name: Juicy Bun Burger 就是棒 美式餐廳 政大店
address: 1F, No.19, Wanshou Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan, 116
opening_hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 10:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 10:30 AM – 10:00 PM
review_1:
I absolutely loved this place! The Chili con Carne Omelette was incredibly delicious, the omelette was so fluffy, and when combined with the rich Pork meat sauces, it became really special and full of flavor. You can tell a lot of care went into making it. I also really enjoyed the bagel, it was fresh and paired perfectly with the meal. The fries were perfectly golden and crispy, and honestly hard to stop eating.
Definitely a spot I’d come back to again!
review_2: Delicious! Portion size was great. We actually weren't able to finish, but the prices were decent! Fried mozzarella cheese sticks were the best! Looking forward to our next visit!
review_3:
Near NCCU, it's where students come for burgers and other dishes! Even though it serves in a not so fancy plates and decorations, the prices are reasonable, and the taste is good. Additionally, there are refillable drinks when you order a set.

I came here during lunch on a weekday, and it was full of people! So, if you need a big table, you can reserve in advance as well.
review_4:
It’s a great place to enjoy the fat food!😆
Burgers are yum and juicy. Beverages are unlimited. Staff are friendly.
Only one thing could be better…
Staff ask us to move our seats for other customers while we are eating. However, no one help us move our things.
review_5: It is a nice place for localized burgers in a college town, lots of energy and young crowd. I found out Taiwanese like their bacon “half cooked” compared to what people do in the States. The bacon needs to be crispy with a beautiful dark brown color in a bacon cheese burger.

address: 1F, No.207, Zhinan Rd, Section 2, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan, 116
opening_hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:30 – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:30 – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:30 – 8:00 PM | Friday: 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:30 – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:30 – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:30 – 8:00 PM
review_1: Best tonkotsu I’ve ever tried in Taipei so far! Extremely crispy, juicy, with thick slab of meat😍 Overall very fulfilling, with free flow of rice, various tea, and miso soup😋 Although the price is quite high, but trust me it’s worth it, CP value is high！Bosses are also really nice and considerate. I ate 2 days straight in a row because it was too tasty!
review_2: Extra-large takeout bento box! Includes main course, shredded cabbage, soft-boiled egg, pickled radish, kimchi, shredded burdock, Japanese-style salad, rice, miso soup, and dressing for the main course. Super filling and delicious, excellent value for money! Refills of rice and miso soup are available for dine-in.
review_3: Prices are high, cash only, and the taste was just average in my opinion. I would only consider visiting again if there were discounts. However, the atmosphere was nice.
review_4:
Even better than the Leshan food I remember!

The crispy fried pork chop is fantastic in both texture and flavor. A business lunch for 199 yuan – highly recommended!

Now that this place is open, I don't need to go to Fushengting on Zhinan Road anymore.
review_5: Everything from the side dishes to the main courses was delicious and worth the price. The grilled miso halibut was tender and delicate, a rarity in typical Japanese set meals. The fried pork cutlet was crispy on the outside and tender on the inside, better than those from chain restaurants, and the portion of tartar sauce was generous.

mandarin_name: 舒曼六號餐館 動物園政大店
address: No. 6, Wanshou Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan 116
opening_hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM
review_1:
Came to NCCU to visit a friend and she brought me to this amazing brunch place which i really was amazed by their delicious food and the interior design from the inside out!! It has a very artsy, rock n roll kind of musician style, the lighting and everything in it makes the entire ambiance of having a brunch inside perfectly match with a sunday afternoon mood. We ordered a Schumann’s brunch (comes with ice americano which also quite good), an energy rice bowl(spice chicken breast tastes not spicy at all but the mayo sauce mixed into the rice was really fantastic!!) and also a chicken kebab.
Overall, it was a great experience here. The staffs are quite busy talking to each other and doing their own business but i think it’s fine… Definitely coming back for the FOOD 😜
review_2:
Not recommend, not authentic, not worth time and money.

Risotto 3/10, dry, bland and most disrespectful and not acceptable is the rice their use isnt RISOTTO rice. You can't use white rice to make risotto.

Doner Kebab 2/10, Döner is my most favorite food in the world. It's farrrrrrrrrr to be called döner kebab. I am not sure if it's fusion or not, but it is a BIG NO NO to order.

Currywurst 6/10, it is in ok and edible level. Fries was the best among foods we ordered.

Mushroom soup 3/10, very watery and almost no taste.

Banana bread 0/10, as a professional baker, this is a HARD ROCK CAFE, I mean it's not edible. DRY AND HARD AS ROCK.

Service girl is ok.. foods was serve after 27 minutes she took our order.

The atmosphere is ok industrial loft decor. But the tables layout is really tight. I almost sit next to another table. I barely can squeeze out.
review_3: Cramped staircase, but the food was excellent. Would definitely come back to try their other dishes. Had their Doner Kebab during my first visit. It was filling and worth every single dollar.
review_4: I had no idea there was a good restaurant near NCCU, right across from the campus. I ordered the pork knuckle risotto set, which came with a cream soup. The taste was great, and it’s definitely good value for the price! I’d love to come back and try more of their dishes. I remember really enjoying my lunch here.
review_5: Was expecting authentic Bavarian pork Knockles from the shop’s name so was disappointed when it came off the bone. Small portion size, nicely flavoured but no authenticity at all. Very cosy setting, tables are very close together.

mandarin_name: 小尚品精制鍋物-達賢館（政大店）
english_name: Xiao Shang Pin Premium Hot Pot – Daxian Branch (NCCU Store)
address: Room 2, No. 36, Wanshou Rd., Zhengda Village (1F, Room 01B01, Daxian Library), Wenshan District, Taipei City 116
opening_hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM
review_1:
This dish is particularly popular in Taiwanese restaurants and in Asia in general.

The space is small, but the layout is quite sensibly designed.

The vegetables and meat are fresh; you can almost tell they don't use frozen food. The dipping sauce isn't particularly varied, but there are still enough ingredients for customers to customize it to their liking. The milk tea is flavorful, if a bit sweet. The black tea is truly aromatic and delicious.

The ice cream display has a small problem: it's quite messy, and after customers have received their ice cream, it often looks quite messy.

The biggest drawback, however, is that, despite being located on a university campus, the staff speaks very little English. This is extremely difficult for Europeans like us, because even the menu isn't in English, the sauces aren't labeled in English, and we're forced to use a translation device or taste everything before making the exact choice we want.

We hardly find any vegetarian dishes here. It's all about hot pot.

There are plenty of parking spaces or paid parking spaces available.

The space is small, certainly not convenient if you have active children.

And wheelchairs are also very difficult to maneuver in this space.

This place doesn't charge fees for transfers or card payments; most payments are made in cash.

But even so, I'm still willing to come back here or introduce friends if I get the chance.

One more thing to note: If you're a student at the school, you'll get a discount or at least some great offers.
review_2: The ingredients are fresh, the meat plate has a lot of meat, the vegetable plate is mainly vegetables, and dumplings need to be ordered separately, so it tastes very healthy XD
review_3: The restaurant was clean and the seating was fairly comfortable. I personally felt the portions of vegetables and meat were quite generous, and the complimentary clams were a nice touch. For $295, it's excellent value for money. There's convenient indoor paid parking downstairs. Currently, they only accept cash and Taiwan Pay.
review_4:
Located downstairs in the Daxian Library at National Chengchi University~

📍Restaurant Environment and Service
The space isn't large, and the tables and sauce area are quite cramped, but there are still plenty of seats, haha. The staff are very friendly, but it seems like they're short-staffed. So when calling to make a reservation, it always takes several tries to get through 🤣

📍Food
This time I ordered the pork hot pot, and with my student ID, I got an extra plate of meat! Although the pork was a bit thin, the extra chicken was quite substantial. The broth was good, the side dishes were mainly whole vegetables, and the udon noodles were similar to those at hot pot noodle shops. They have sauces, drinks, and ice cream, so I think it's great value for money!
review_5:
Located on the first floor of the National Chengchi University Library, this place has a fresh, modern decor with a small pond in front, creating a wonderful atmosphere.

The staff are friendly and courteous.

Xiao Shangpin always comes with a meat plate when you order two hot pots.

The plate only contains vegetables, which is very healthy and fresh.

I wouldn't recommend the pork or chicken! The quality needs improvement; both have a meaty smell.

The marinated beef is delicious.

The drinks and ice cream at the buffet are pretty good.

address: 2F, No.32, Zhinan Rd, Section 2, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan, 116
opening_hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed
review_1: Nice food!
review_2: Small amount of food. Taste is average.
review_3:
Visited during dinner time on a weekday.
The Mexican cream pasta with a mild spicy flavor was quite delicious. The apple sparkling drink with agar jelly wasn’t overly sweet, and the large size was very refreshing.
They also accept electronic payment, which is very convenient.
review_4:
This place has a really great location! It’s up a rather inconspicuous staircase right next to the main gate of National Chengchi University. It’s a nice spot to spend some time, and I’m sure students at NCCU are quite familiar with it.
That day, I ordered the safer choice — pesto pasta. Unfortunately, the pasta was a bit too firm, and the sauce tasted fairly ordinary without anything particularly memorable.
review_5:
The white wine clam spaghetti was delicious and great value for money.

The honey ice cream was also good, but the portion was a bit small.
I'd definitely add $10 for a full serving of honey ice cream 🥹

(The richest honey ice cream I've ever had!)

I saw my friend eating Mexican sauce with fried chicken thighs, and it looked pretty good too.

I'd like to try it next time! Perfect for a meal after class with friends.

english_name: Zheng Dayuan Zhi Beef Noodle Restaurant
address: No. 59, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan 116011
opening_hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM
review_1: The photo shows what supposed to be a bigger portion and that costs you additional 50 dollars. Just doesn’t make sense. The beef is ok and the broth is on the oily side. Free soybean milk is fancy and there are quite a few other free add-ons. I don’t feel it justifies the price.
review_2:
Customers are expected to read the instructions and place their orders at the counter themselves, while the staff are responsible for serving the dishes and clearing the tables. There wasn’t any particularly notable service, so I gave the service category 3 stars.
For the atmosphere, the place was clean and tidy with bright lighting. The music volume was moderate, and the tables and chairs were arranged comfortably without feeling crowded. Overall, it was quite pleasant, so I gave this category 4 stars.
As for the food, the wide noodles were cooked to the perfect texture — neither too soft nor too firm — and had a pleasant wheat aroma 👍. The clear-broth beef soup was rich, flavorful, and naturally sweet, yet not overly heavy; even after finishing the whole bowl, I didn’t feel thirsty 👍👍. The portion of beef felt just right — satisfying enough, while still leaving you wanting to come back again next time. The meat was tender with a nice balance of lean meat and a little bit of fat for extra flavor. Absolutely delicious 👍👍👍.
The side dishes were a bit disappointing in comparison. Because the beef and soup were so good, the braised egg only felt average — neither bad nor particularly memorable. The tofu was cut in a standard way, not the thinly sliced type that’s extra flavorful and addictive. As for the kelp, it was over-braised, too soft, and tasted overly salty.
review_3: The broth was excellent, but the noodles were just average, the beef portion was a bit small, and the price was on the expensive side. Overall, the business was good, with a constant stream of customers. The owner looked fierce, but the service was quite good. The environment was simply and cleanly decorated, with old-fashioned bowls, rattan chairs, and stainless steel folding tables giving it a slightly rustic feel.
review_4:
Clear Beef Noodle Soup 4/5: The broth was very fragrant and not overly salty.
Side Dishes 3/5: Very ordinary.
Soy milk and black tea were available; the soy milk was decent, but the black tea was watery.
Environment 3/5: Clean.
review_5: Overall, the taste was acceptable; the beef had a moderate texture, but the broth was a bit bland. Soy milk, black tea, and various condiments were available.

mandarin_name: 小木屋鬆餅 政治大學店
english_name: Shine Mood Waffle National Chengchi University
address: No. 97, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan 11651
opening_hours: Monday: 10:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 10:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 10:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 10:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 10:30 AM – 8:00 PM
review_1: I tried the savory one, and it went beyond my expectation. The chicken meat is tender and juicy. Highly rec.
review_2: I first tried this waffle shop inside NTU and I liked it. This specific shop has seats with air condition and restroom inside. It might get packed during school days. Their waffles and drinks are very good with a student-friendly price. Their chicken nuggets honestly tastes better than McDonald's. Their menu has English and foreign and local credit cards are accepted.
review_3: If you have sweet taste or miss the pancakes, just come here and order many favors you want, including sweet and salty favors. :)
review_4: Very nice waffles for a cheap price. There are a lot of different options for salty or sugary waffles. Note that there are a lot of people at lunch.
review_5: The regular waffles are delicious and has a really nice value, but the seasonal waffles are mediocre. The waffles for the strawberry with cream waffles (NT 99) in season right now doesn't seem freshly cooked and the portion is smaller than the regular waffles as they only use half a waffle but the strawberries and filling is pretty decent.

address: No. 12, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan 116
opening_hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 10:30 PM | Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 10:30 PM | Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 10:30 PM | Thursday: 11:30 AM – 10:30 PM | Friday: 11:30 AM – 10:30 PM | Saturday: 11:30 AM – 10:30 PM | Sunday: 11:30 AM – 10:30 PM
review_1: Little bit expensive for students, but recommended to try at least once. Free refill of drink and ice cream, the steak taste is good, but expect to get larger portion.
review_2: Amazing black pepper sauce
review_3: One of my favorite places to eat in campus! This restaurant offers quick and generous meals for hungry students. The staff are very welcoming and friendly.
review_4: Good , delicious and reasonable affordable price,
review_5: Excellent.  I like their soup

address: No. 86, Baoyi Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan 116
opening_hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 9:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 9:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 9:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 9:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 9:30 AM – 8:00 PM
review_1: Pretty authentic Vietnamese food in Taipei! The Vietnamese pancakes and bun cha are our absolute favorites. The atmosphere could use a little help — would be even nicer if they tidied up a bit — but overall, still a tasty spot worth trying.
review_2: Not very authentic Vietnamese cuisine. We ordered lemongrass chicken rice (香茅雞腿飯), but there wasn't even a hint of lemongrass, and the sauce seemed to be missing the essential ingredients that define this dish. We queried this with the owner, but they insisted that there had been no mistake with the ordering (the dish was how they intended it to be). Another curry dish we ordered seemed to be flavourless. Vegetables advertised to be prepared with fish sauce 魚露 and fish paste seemed to have neither added, and were overcooked to the point of being relatively flavourless (unless one counts oil as a flavour). We couldn't fault the price or the portion size, and left the restaurant feeling full, but the overall feeling was a long way away from traditional Vietnamese cooking.
review_3: Pho was ok, I had seafood version. Just found only 1 shrimp in it. Felt like I am eating only the pho noodles and the soup. They can do something in adding some real meat in Their pho.
review_4: A little greasy spoon you wouldn't think to walk into, but cheap, delicious, and worth it if you do. Remember to ask for a Vietnamese coffee. It's not on the menu, but they'll sort you out with one all the same.
review_5: Vietnamese sandwiches are pretty good, even if it's nothing like the real thing. Pho isn't too bad either. Quite reasonably priced.

mandarin_name: 蛋蛋的幸福 蔬食料理
address: No. 25, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan 116
opening_hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed
review_1:
We ordered rice with cheese and black coffee. The food is great, beyond average, the volume of vegetables and it’s taste with rice and cheese could fit its price (90-140). But, maybe the price is a bit higher for average citizens in Taipei. Then the drinks could balance it as I think is 10-20 lower than the peers.
The space is very limited but atmosphere is good with light music and the boss is very nice though quiet.
Will recommend this restaurant with score 4.
review_2:
If you're looking for vegetarian food, this is the perfect place! It's a small cafe in a side street near Muzha market but totally worth a visit.
The owners are very friendly people and go out of their way to make you feel comfortable and happy. They always put a smile on my face :)
The cafe is also not too far away from NCCU campus, so it's easy to go for a nice lunch in-between classes.
If you don't speak Chinese, ask them for a English menu which they will kindly provide for you.
review_3:
Super high quality ingredients and real cheese. The price is totally justified because they really don't skimp on the cheese, vegetables, and tofu. Definitely not one of those places with 99% noodles/rice and 1% good food. Extremely filling.
Highly recommend, try the 咖哩蓋飯，you won't regret it.
review_4: Best veggie place around NCCU campus for sure. Mixing food from different horizons while keeping high quality ingredients and cooking quality, served by a lovely staff. Only thing to mind might be the price of at least 150NTD for a dish.
review_5: Delicious vegetarian cafe with good quality ingredients and friendly owners! Both the curry fried rice and white curry were delicious! Plenty of fresh vegetables, tofu and nuts. Would like to come back to try the sandwiches next time.

address: No. 34-1, Section 1, Xinguang Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan 116
opening_hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 7:30 AM – 3:30 PM | Wednesday: 7:30 AM – 3:30 PM | Thursday: 7:30 AM – 3:30 PM | Friday: 7:30 AM – 3:30 PM | Saturday: 7:30 AM – 3:30 PM | Sunday: 7:30 AM – 3:30 PM
review_1:
2022.12.02
▶️Quality: the salad was fresh with enough dressing (many brunch restaurants tend to use as few as possible)
The potato was sweet and well steamed.
The mashed potato was normal.
The omelette was surprisingly good as there were mozzarella and corns wrapped inside!
Unlike other similar brunch restaurants, the sweetness and ice are adjustable for your beverage here!

▶️Price: $95 for veggie set. Inexpensive based on how much work it requires to make such good meal.

▶️Environment: comfy, clean and bright with AC on.

▶️Service: fast and friendly, three staffs at the counter checking the situations.

▶️Line Pay available.
review_2: Wrap is seldom seen in Taiwan breakfast shop nearby nccu, the taste is not bad too
review_3:
love this place!
Fast and friendly service,food is delicious!
review_4: Just a generic student breakfast and lunch place. It is not expensive: you can get a meal for 80-130 NTD dollars. The service is fast even when the restaurant is crowded. But unfortunekty  the quality of the food is not hight at all. They also serve teas which are so sweet it is impossible to drink them.
review_5:
I unexpectedly found a delicious breakfast shop near National Chengchi University. The restaurant is clean and well-maintained, and the modern interior design is exactly the style I like.
Most importantly, the owner and staff were very warm, friendly, and polite. It really made my day a happy one 😄

english_name: TDH Mao Cha Ting Afternoon Tea
address: No. 117, Baoyi Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan 11650
opening_hours: Monday: 10:30 AM – 7:30 PM | Tuesday: 10:30 AM – 7:30 PM | Wednesday: 10:30 AM – 7:30 PM | Thursday: 10:30 AM – 7:30 PM | Friday: 10:30 AM – 7:30 PM | Saturday: 10:30 AM – 7:30 PM | Sunday: 10:30 AM – 7:30 PM
review_1:
I was expecting to dine in as the name of this “restaurant “ was “Tea Time” but turned out it was a dessert shop only for taking out.

The staff was friendly and straightforward and gave me the exact advice I needed. Good service!

The mochi was tasty and full of tea flavor so I came here to buy more after receiving the gift 🤣

There are so many tea related products to try… gonna come again next time!
review_2: Tea and desserts that are created in perfect harmony. Definitely not too sweet. The tea flavors are very strong but not bitter. The chocolate souffle is decadent and creamy. The ice cream is good too. Well done! It's a shame anyone complains about the price.
review_3: Prob the best dessert place in Wenshan Dist.
review_4: This is Muzha's signatory tea flavored dessert store. They provide wide range of desserts including chocolate, Swiss roll, cheesecake, puffs, mochi, soufflé, and ice cream. Each kind has several tea flavors to select from. Their desserts are very enjoyable, but the indoor sitting is not very well-organised. I usually do takeaways.
review_5: Jogging through this road everyday, I though it was a tea shop or cafe, turns out that it’s a dessert shop, great tea flavor dessert, will visit here more often.

address: No. 73, Baoyi Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan 116
review_1:
Visited: 2024/06/14 (Fri)
Ordered:
- 暖呼呼叉燒鷄白湯拉麵(Piping Hot Chashu Chicken Paitan Ramen) — 160 NTD
- 豬鷄繁盛(Deluxe Pork & Chicken Topping Combo) - 60 NTD

Wait time: 0 min (1P, weekday, evening)

Positive: Clean and cheap. Japanese shichimi powder is provided.

Negative: Quite watery and tasteless, just exactly how Taiwanese people like it.

Good option if you just want to eat budget ramen and there is no other decent ramen nearby.

Final Score: 5/10
review_2: I originally just wanted to come in for a bowl of ramen to warm my stomach, but this restaurant's menu is cursed! It ruined our diet, sorry wallet! To pretend we were eating a balanced diet, we ordered grilled zucchini, baby corn, and asparagus, plus shredded chicken crisps. Every dish was delicious, and the whole dining experience was fantastic!
review_3:
This time, I went to Mian Kuang Kuang Ramen and chose their signature tonkotsu ramen. Overall, the ramen here is quite refreshing, unlike the rich and salty traditional Japanese tonkotsu ramen, making it suitable for those who like broth.

🍜 Ramen Texture
I chose the thick noodles this time, but they were a bit firm, perhaps because the restaurant prefers a chewier texture. Next time, I'll choose a softer texture, which would be more suitable. The broth is a refreshing tonkotsu soup, not greasy, which suits Taiwanese tastes. However, those accustomed to the richer Japanese tonkotsu soup might find it a little bland.

🥩 Toppings and Side Dishes
The char siu pork was good, with some fat but not too greasy, and the meat was tender and flavorful. The signature karaage chicken was crispy on the outside and tender on the inside, fried just right without being greasy. The soft-boiled egg was also delicious, with the yolk cooked to perfection, rich and slightly runny. What's particularly special are the winter melon and baby corn; these two side dishes are very refreshing and perfectly complement the ramen, cutting through the richness – a thoughtful combination.

💡 Summary and Impressions: Men Kuang Kuang's tonkotsu ramen has a lighter, more refreshing style, suitable for those who like broth but don't like overly salty flavors. The firmness of the noodles could be adjusted, but the side dishes and fried items are quite good. Overall, it's a ramen shop worth trying. If I visit again, I'll try different noodle firmness to see if it suits my taste better!
review_4:
Aside from beef noodle shops, this newly opened Mian Kuang Kuang is probably the most expensive noodle shop in Muzha.

However, it's worth the price.

While the ambiance isn't like Ichiran Ramen in Japan, the customization options are quite similar. Besides the thickness, portion size, and firmness of the noodles, there are various toppings to choose from, including whether to add scallions and garlic. The shop also offers free lemon water and chicken broth for customers to add themselves.

For my first time, I ordered the tonkotsu ramen, choosing the thin noodles recommended by the shop. The firmness and toppings were normal, without scallions. The tonkotsu broth looked rich, but it was surprisingly refreshing and not too salty—easy to drink the whole bowl. The medium-firm thin noodles were chewy and absorbed the broth well. The chashu pork, although only two slices and quite thin, was still tasty and not too greasy. The only slightly unusual thing was the vegetables: a few slices of dried bamboo shoots, a typical ramen accompaniment, but the baby corn and broccoli, which are unlikely to be cooked too soft, had a slightly firm texture that didn't quite balance the overall flavor of the bowl. However, the noodles were quite delicious overall.

The shop was very clean, and the staff were very polite and attentive. Although the space wasn't large, the seating was fairly spacious and comfortable.

Overall, a bowl of ramen priced between 135 and 250 yuan is indeed on the expensive side in Muzha. But today's Muzha is no longer the low-end area traditionally associated with military personnel and government employees. New high-rise buildings starting at over 30 million yuan are commonplace, and many residents drive imported luxury cars. However, the quality of food in Muzha hasn't kept pace with the improvement in residents' economic capabilities.

Therefore, a modern and refined noodle shop like Mian Kuang Kuang, even in its trial operation phase, seems to have a lot of customers, indicating that Muzha residents are willing to accept mid-to-high-end dining options. Other mid-to-high-end chain restaurants should follow suit and open branches in Muzha.
review_5:
Overall, it was quite delicious!

Today I ordered tonkotsu ramen, milk ramen, and mentaiko (spicy pork) rice bowl; all were tasty. The restaurant has limited seating, but the small rooms are cozy.

review_1: My favourite breakfast spot in the whole of Taipei. A local restaurant ran by the nicest couple I’ve ever met. I come here every morning and never once had a single complaint. Very good prices and very good fresh food. Couldn’t recommend it enough :)
review_2: great
review_3:
The toast is so soft and fluffy! I don't usually eat pork belly, but this was amazing. It had absolutely no fatty texture, tasting like grilled pork from a Korean restaurant. I love ordering everything separately and then assembling it into my own personalized toast flavor, topped with chili sauce and soy sauce. The egg pancake is also fantastic—not oily at all. Whenever I'm craving breakfast, I always stop by here first. It's become one of my go-to breakfast spots at my hotel in Muzha!

I like sitting at a table with friends because I can see the people and cars on the street and the changing sky.
review_4:
I've been eating this since college, and I still often come back for the simple, delicious taste of it, just to get a raise sooner 🤤

🍳Pork Tenderloin Omelet + Cabbage $70

🍳Pork Tenderloin Sandwich $65 Pork tenderloin is an item I've repurchased countless times. The difference lies in the type of starch, and the cabbage shreds in the omelet are pan-fried. If I want a rich, crispy toast and refreshing cabbage shreds, I'll have the sandwich; if I want chili sauce and a pretty cross-section of the omelet, I'll have the omelet, haha.

🍳Yuzu Pepper Chicken Sandwich $65 This is a relatively rare breakfast flavor. It's a mildly seasoned meat, but you can taste the yuzu pepper aroma as you chew. I choose this when I want something lighter.

🍳Tomato Cheese Sandwich + Bacon $70 A surprisingly great combination! It's like a much bigger, upgraded version of the MOS Tomato Cheese Egg Burger! Chop the tomatoes, mix them with the egg, add fresh shredded cabbage, freshly fried bacon, and rich cheese slices. The toast is also toasted on a griddle until slightly crispy. It's a nutritionally balanced and incredibly satisfying treat! 🥹

🍳 Honey Leather French Toast $35 I'd definitely order an extra one if I wanted something sweet! Cheese sandwiched in toast, dipped in egg, pan-fried until lightly browned, and drizzled with honey—it's absolutely amazing! I'd order it every time if I had a bigger stomach.

🍳 Handmade Radish Cake $40 You can taste individual strands of radish, and it doesn't have dried shrimp (I personally prefer radish cake without shrimp, haha), which allows you to really taste the natural sweetness of the radish. It's also delicious with soy sauce and chili sauce 😌

🍳 Fried Egg (Market Price, I've had it a few times so far, always $10) Being able to get a hot, freshly fried half-boiled/fully cooked sunny-side-up egg for ten dollars is pure bliss... I always order one or two 🤤

🍳 Fresh Milk Tea $30 The black tea is only slightly sweet, and with Ah Hou's fresh milk, it's refreshing and not too sweet.

🍳 Unsweetened Soy Milk The $20 soy milk is also handpicked by the owners; it's thicker than what you'd find at other breakfast shops. I always order either fresh milk tea or soy milk, and they always fill my own cup to the brim! 🥹

What I love about this breakfast spot is the owners' attention to detail. They carefully select the toast, pomelo pepper chicken, homemade radish cake, soy milk, and Ah Hou fresh milk. Plus, the owners are very friendly and provide excellent service; the warm and welcoming atmosphere feels just like home.

The food isn't greasy, and the environment is clean and bright. ✨ I often have it for lunch. Eating freshly made toast/egg pancakes with a cup of soy milk always makes me feel so happy! review_2: The roast meats came with a nice mung bean soup. I ordered the $90 char siu and spare ribs combo, and the char siu was really good. The spare ribs were a bit soft, perhaps because they were cold, and the flavor wasn't too strong, but the sweetness and sauce concentration slightly affected the texture of the meat. Overall, for $90, it's pretty good value for money. The owner checked my portion size before serving the food, which was thoughtful.
review_3:
Three Treasures Rice $100
Arriving after peak meal times, the rice was hot, but the vegetables and meat were cold. I'm fine with that, but the spareribs and barbecued pork were quite tough. The chicken was okay, and the sausage was the best. However, I think the biggest letdown was the sauce. It was so thick and salty, like a grease, that it felt like a soup, ruining the overall experience.
review_4:
Having lived in Wenshan District for a while, I wouldn't say I've explored it all, but I generally know or have been to most of the well-known places; otherwise, I've just passed by and looked away... and there are quite a few of those. So I still have to rely on Google to find out what's possible. During the peak of summer last year, I randomly found this place and decided to check it out.

Because it was outside the main gate of National Chengchi University, and it was during the holidays, even at lunchtime, the shop was dark and looked like it wasn't open. After going in, I had to call out before I saw the owner. He told me to wait a moment, that the food would be ready soon. He probably mainly caters to students on weekdays, so there are fewer people during the summer holidays, and he doesn't need to prepare food as actively.

When the owner was serving the food, he asked if I wanted more rice, and I said no. He probably thought I was a glutton, but I'm actually just a small bowl of rice; I don't like rice at all. Also, since it's a student business, he knows that boys that age have big appetites, and more carbohydrates would definitely fill them up. That said, when I got home and opened the bento box, there was still a lot of rice. The whole bento box looked like a can of bloated herring, all bulging out. The side dishes also filled up the food compartments. It was another owner who worried I hadn't eaten enough—these kinds of owners, located near schools, are truly compassionate and don't care about the cost, focusing on making sure you're full. But I've been out of that mindset for a long time. If it were handmade noodles, I'd be happy to eat my fill! Haha.

Actually, looking at the owner, I thought he'd be retired by the time I wrote this food review a year later. Because when we were chatting, he told me he was taking a vacation in a few days and didn't want me to come for nothing (after all, he's not a digital person and can't use Facebook or Google Maps). He was going to the US for his son's graduation ceremony, saying it was his first time traveling abroad. Seeing his expression and demeanor, even as a stranger, I felt genuinely happy; that overflowing parental love was palpable. Running a small eatery near a school, sending their children abroad to study—the hard work and pride should be written all over their faces, especially with such incredibly generous prices.

The food here is fairly average, the kind of Taiwanese roast meat you'd find in Taipei. Nothing particularly outstanding, but nothing to criticize either—of course, this isn't comparing it to what you'd find in Hong Kong. However, buying a bento box reminded me of the warmth and sincerity of people, giving me a different feeling on a sweltering midday. More than a year later, as I write this food review, it's still open.

More food reviews on Google: Nanhe Zazu
review_5:
This roast meat shop is probably one of the most frequented by students in the neighborhood. It's delicious, inexpensive, and you can eat your fill! The owner even gives you a bottle of Yakult with each meal. When serving your rice, the owner will ask if you've eaten enough, and if you have too much, you can change the vegetables. It's really great!

Three Treasures Rice (90 RMB) This place's Three Treasures Rice is fantastic. With three kinds of main dishes, you get to taste all kinds of meat, plus plenty of vegetables. The portion is generous, it's delicious, and the owner is super nice. Highly recommended!

review_3:
Environment: Old and unkempt; air conditioning was advertised but not turned on, making it stuffy.

Service: People who seemed to be family members sat chatting near the entrance, but the food preparation was incomplete, resulting in a long wait after ordering.

Braised Pork Rice: Not real braised pork; the sauce was just served directly on top of the rice, and it was too salty.

Beef Noodles: A large bowl of noodles came with more than 7 or 8 slices of beef. For the price of 100 yuan, it was acceptable.
review_4:
Wonton Soup (Small) 30 Yuan
The small bowl of wonton soup came with 10 wontons, which was quite a bit of a splurge. It's a very affordable food stall price, but the taste... The wonton wrappers were a bit thick, the filling wasn't fishy, ​​and the pork was a bit fatty. The flavor was just average, which is something I'm not very experienced with. The soup is a simple combination of crispy shallots and green onions, without MSG, making it very healthy.

Boiled Dumplings 4 Yuan each
I really don't recommend the cabbage; it's dry and tough, and the pork is seasoned with ginger to remove the fishy smell. The filling is also fatty and pork-heavy, which is a shame. The lack of MSG was a shame, but I couldn't taste the sweetness of the cabbage or pork. The chive dumplings were okay, but the pork filling was too fatty, so I wasn't a fan.

Beef Noodles (Small) 70 Yuan
The broth was average, the beef was not springy and tough, and the noodles were just average. However, the price was affordable and it was a good place to fill your stomach.

Side dishes and dried tofu
Affordable side dishes, three for only 10 yuan, which I honestly think is a good deal. However, when it comes to braised dishes, I really care about the soy sauce. It's not special; it's just your average soy sauce, a bit salty and average.
review_5: The Lu Rou Fan contains dried tofu, so it's like a rice version of Zha Jiang Noodles. It's just the right amount of salty, and goes well with rice. The beef noodles contain pickled cabbage and are flavored with spices like star anise. The portions were quite generous and delicious. It feels like a community-based restaurant that attracts locals.

address: No. 17, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan 116
review_1: Super nice owner, fresh tasty meat and vegetables, good value for money 👍🏼
review_2:
The hot pot dishes were incredibly generous with ingredients and inexpensive; each pot was overflowing, so filling you'd be stuffed! 🥰 You wouldn't even need noodles to finish it all. The vegetables were all excellent, and the corn was super sweet! 💪🏻

📍The Mushroom, Mustard Green, and Black Chicken Hot Pot ($379) was quite unique. The broth was light and flavorful, the mustard greens were a delightful bittersweet taste, and there was a generous amount of black chicken.

📍The Braised Beef Brisket Hot Pot ($359) also had a lot of meat, but unfortunately, the overall flavor was a bit one-dimensional. Next time, I'd like to order the sauerkraut and pork belly hot pot or the sesame oil chicken, which my family highly recommends. 🤭
review_3:
This was my first time stepping into this restaurant during my four years of university. The menu was unique; many dishes that would normally be served in large pots for multiple people were served individually – a lifesaver for those homesick while away from home.

I originally wanted to order the fish hot pot, but it was sold out, so I opted for the lamb hot pot instead. Having grown up with small legs, I'm very picky about lamb.

To my surprise, the lamb, with its classic flavor, was perfectly stewed – tender yet firm, flavorful without being greasy. It had a subtle milky aroma and almost no gamey taste, a testament to the restaurant's fresh ingredients.

Secondly, the selection of hot pot ingredients was also ingenious. The tofu tasted like handmade tofu, fluffy and creamy with a subtle bean flavor. The duck meatballs were very firm, unlike the starchy meatballs commonly found in Taipei; they were chewy and springy.

And it's worth mentioning that the restaurant meticulously removed the tough stems from the cabbage.

The broth was relatively light, but definitely flavorful. The broth is bursting with the restaurant's proud, naturally flavorful bone broth, complemented by scallions, corn, and cabbage, with a subtly sweet taste.

I wish I'd discovered it sooner! I'll definitely be back!
review_4:
December 1st. I went to get a haircut yesterday, and the owner recommended this place. Today it got cooler, so I thought I'd have hot pot and came here.

It was unexpectedly delicious—a surprise, like discovering a hidden gem! The pickled cabbage and pork belly hot pot I ordered had very tasty and fresh pickled cabbage and pork belly. I wanted to take the soup base home, but I was too embarrassed to say anything. Personally, I think it's far superior to Qian Du.

The drinks were also better than Qian Du's. I had the unsweetened oolong tea, and it was a pleasant oolong with a slight aftertaste, not the bitter kind. The dipping sauces were also better than Qian Du's, with fresh scallions, chili peppers, etc. (Qian Du's were all frozen in the refrigerator, I don't know how long). This place even had sesame paste; the owner must be a connoisseur. And then there's the heating system—using a gas stove and a fire—it's rare to find a place that uses fire for heating. I think this kind of fire-heated hot pot is what makes it a proper hot pot experience. It makes me want to try other types of pots.
review_5:
🔸Pickled Cabbage and Pork Belly Hot Pot: The pickled cabbage broth is fantastic. Initially, there's a lot of pickled cabbage, so it's a bit sour. I recommend adding more cabbage to balance the sourness with its sweetness, which makes the broth even better!! The ingredients are plentiful: pork belly, frozen tofu, bean curd sheets, shrimp 🦐, hot pot ingredients, duck blood, enoki mushrooms—you can't possibly finish them all! For this price, the individual hot pot ingredients are far superior to other hot pot restaurants…

🔸Mushroom, Clam, and Chicken Hot Pot: Plenty of chicken thigh meat, cabbage, and clams—a very generous serving! After finishing the ingredients, the broth is perfect for cooking Korean ramen…

☑️The dining environment is clean and bright, the staff are friendly, and the self-service bar is unlimited. Reservations are recommended for dining times…

☑️A hot pot restaurant with incredibly high cost-performance…

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️A great place for family and friends to dine and gather. Worth trying and visiting again!!

So delicious, I'll definitely come again!

The grilled squid sausage was amazing, with a slightly crispy exterior and a perfectly chewy interior, and a rich aroma. The bacon and scallops, grilled yam and mentaiko, salt-grilled baby corn, and salt-grilled chicken cartilage were all super delicious.

The Japanese fried noodles were tasty, but I don't like mayonnaise, so it was pretty good without it. I saw people recommending the century egg and stinky tofu, but I personally didn't like it.

We ordered too much this time, and were almost full after only half the meal. The skewers came later, and we also ordered some beer. Overall, it was very delicious and satisfying. The portions weren't large, and the price was reasonable, but the dishes were quite exquisite. I was very satisfied with my first time trying it, and I admire the owner's ability to create such delicious dishes from various ingredients in such a short time. The dining and cooking areas shared the same space, but there was no smell of cooking oil. Next time, I'd like to try the grilled fish and other drinks.

There aren't many seats, so the owner said it's best to call and make a reservation.

review_1: The broth, chili, and ingredients are all incredibly delicious! The food is so comforting and easy on the stomach, and you can really taste the heart and soul the owner puts into the cooking. It’s a cozy little gem that I’ll definitely be back to visit!
review_2: This place needs no introduction. It’s an absolute legend at NCCU! I could seriously eat here 10 times a week. Love the owner! ❤️
review_3: The owner is super handsome, and the food is absolutely incredible! 🔥
review_4:
I missed it so much after graduating for over two years that I came back for dinner!

It was just as delicious as I remembered! (Highly recommend the eggs and radish; I order them almost every time. And if you can handle spice, definitely add extra chili!)

review_2: The restaurant is nearby NCCU, Thai food taste good but it not sour and spicy as the local Thai food, perharps for fill the Taiwanese’s taste. In general, I reccomend this restaurant for the affordable price, friendly staff and great service.

review_1: First time trying this spot! The owner was already super busy right when they opened at 5:00 PM, and I had to wait 20 minutes after ordering. It was also my first time having fried rice topped with mayonnaise and pork floss. I never expected this combination to be so incredibly delicious! 😍 One bite after another, and before I knew it, half of it was already gone. The portion size is huge, and it's definitely value for money! 👍
review_2: Delicious! Highly recommended! The young owner was very polite! The proprietress was very friendly!
review_3: Such a lovely little shop—it's a cozy place with quite a story behind it! They originally started out selling Taiwanese vermicelli, but at some point, they began offering fried rice as well, available in both white pepper and soy sauce flavors. For students, it is an absolute steal! The owner takes extra good care of students. 🥰 Not only do students get free extra rice, but if you dine in, they also serve a complimentary bowl of hot soup to warm your stomach. 🥰🥰 I truly felt so well taken care of. 🙂‍↕️🙂‍↕️
review_4: The beef was delicious! I've also tried the XO sauce seafood fried rice. The rice was very good, but the shrimp was bland and seemed out of place with the delicious fried rice.
review_5: It’s been a long time since I’ve had fried rice this good! Ordered the beef fried rice, XO sauce fried rice, and Hawaiian fried rice. The rice grains are perfectly distinct and packed with that amazing 'wok hei' (wok aroma). They are super generous with the ingredients—every single bite is full of toppings. You can really taste the care put into the food. Highly recommend this great spot in Muzha! 🍳🔥

address: No. 31, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan 116
review_1: The food is delicious although the service isn’t good 😅
review_2: The lady who runs the restaurant has very very bad attitude and poor service-minded. She scolds me because I wrote Korean menu name unbeautifully as she likes. She shows unhappy face when she has overwhelming customers. She seems like she doesn’t want to welcome customers so I wonder why she open the restaurant. She is a Korean who lives in another country so she should even be more behave and nicer to people more than this. I will not recommend this restaurant and I will not go back there again!!!
review_3: Food taste so good, but wait too long
review_4: When I saw an actual menu in Korean for once,  I guessed the food must be more authentic than most other places. Definitely worth a return visit. Just be prepared to wait a bit longer than would usually need to. The Ajumma was also authentic: No Prisoners taken.
review_5: I had the 五花肉豆腐(Pork Belly with Tofu)，basically pork with tofu soup. Comes with rice and side dish. I'd say this is one of the more authentic Korean near NCCU. One of our favourite place, especially during winter. Their side dishes are really nice too.

address: No. 97, Baoyi Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan 116
opening_hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 1:00 PM | Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 1:00 PM | Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 1:00 PM | Thursday: 7:00 AM – 1:00 PM | Friday: 7:00 AM – 1:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 1:00 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 1:00 PM
review_1: It's a bit far from the bustling Muzha Market. The owner was very friendly, but unfortunately, the cold noodles lacked sesame sauce and garlic flavor; the sauce just tasted like soy sauce and wasn't very delicious. The miso soup was alright. The environment was like a roadside stall, with large flies buzzing around, but I didn't mind, considering the prices were typical roadside stall prices, so there wasn't much to complain about.
review_2: The sesame cold noodles were delicious, not too sour or salty. The meatballs in the miso soup were delicious, as were the egg drop soup. The prices were reasonable, and the owner was very friendly. We had a lot of takeout at noon, so there wasn't much seating inside. There were fans, but no air conditioning. Unfortunately, the restaurant is only open at noon.
review_3: The lady boss is very friendly. The egg drop soup is super delicious! A must-buy! The sesame sauce for the cold noodles would be even better if it were a little more fragrant. The mustard is chemically made, so it's not too pungent. The dining area is open and a bit warm. But for 50 yuan for the cold noodles and 40 yuan for the soup, you're completely full and happy. I'll definitely visit again.
review_4: Business is good. The environment is clean. The staff are friendly. The sauce is rich and fragrant, and adding some spice makes it pretty tasty. The flavor is on the stronger side. The noodles are slightly cool and have a nice texture. The miso soup is good and freshly made. The taste is slightly mild, which pairs perfectly with the cold noodles.
review_5:
On Tuesday, March 14, 2023, after finishing work and returning to Muzha, I suddenly had a craving for cold noodles. I Googled nearby cold noodle shops on my phone and found this one with pretty good reviews, so I rode my bike over to try it. I ordered a large bowl of mixed cold noodles for $55 and a meatball miso soup for $35, totaling $90. The mixed cold noodles included wasabi, and when mixed, the sesame flavor combined with all the seasonings made it quite refreshing. Paired with the standard miso meatball soup, it was a perfect match. The original phrase is "相得益彰" (xiang de yi zhang), where "彰" means obvious or prominent, and "相得益彰" means that they complement and enhance each other, highlighting their respective strengths.

In short, it was OK!

review_1: This is the best place to eat Japanese food at NCCU! Meat and fish is always fresh. The owner and people who work there are so nice. review_2:
I just found out that there’s a Japanese restaurant with a variety of sashimis right across from NCCU!

The quality is not bad at all, and the price is student-friendly! The bowl I ordered in the picture is just 220 NTD!

The miso soup is also unlimited.
review_3: When I have an itchy fu of sushi, this is the place I will go near my home! Very friendly staff and cozy place to eat Japanese food but suggest not to come in rush hour! Come by before 8pm then you will have a seat for sure! No parking by the way, so the closest parking is in the NCCU or Wanxing elementary school parking and then walk there.
review_4: Almost full during our visit, as there were around 20 seats inside. It's a moderate choice, and with this price range, I have a higher expectation. And it's true there are similar choices, with better presentation around this range.
review_5: Free soup refill and water! Love the restaurant the food is literally superb! And filling for its price would totally go back when I visit Taiwan again :)))

review_1: Very delicious ...

review_1: The food is overall delicious, but the turnip cake I got today was a bit too dark. Otherwise, the flavor is actually pretty good! The taro paste and pork floss egg crepe is also amazing, and the milk tea isn't too sweet.
review_2: Seriously delicious! My absolute favorites are the turnip cake triple combo and the meat floss taro paste egg crepe, paired with a milk tea and a hot latte. review_4: Just got an iced latte, and as someone who is super picky about coffee—wow, this is incredibly good! The quality is easily on par with the coffee scene in South Korea! 🤔 I usually get my coffee from 'Stand Up Café' (立良), and this place can absolutely hold its own against them. review_1: This is my third time eating Hainanese chicken rice at this restaurant. This time I had the roasted chicken, and it tasted great. The chicken was very tender, and the side dishes were also very appetizing. Overall, it was delicious.
review_2: The owner was very polite, and the dishes were lightly seasoned, making them perfect for those who prefer a lighter diet. They also offered seafood, a rare find near National Chengchi University.
review_3:
I ordered the pork and meatball dry noodles. It wasn't as dry as I expected, but it was still quite tasty.

The flavor was on the lighter side, and the portion size was average.

However, it was worth it for 60 yuan, as it included two standard-sized pork meatballs.
review_4:
Roasted Hainanese Chicken Rice
The rice was absolutely delicious!!
It was the best rice I've had near National Chengchi University. It was soft and moist, but not mushy. It paired beautifully with the light side dishes and tangy sauce. The stark contrast between the main character and the supporting characters was perfect. The person who cooked the rice was amazing!
review_5:
I really enjoyed the food and the dining environment.

However, I personally hope the tableware can be changed to Taiwanese-made stainless steel or ceramic, because melamine is quite toxic, and I noticed that many of the tableware pieces are already severely worn.

I think improving this would be better for customers' health, and it would also allow me to dine in more often 🙏. (Especially since I almost always order hot soup…)

review_2: The male cashiers are always friendly, but for some reason, they never speak when paying the female cashiers. They're always so friendly to the previous customer, but when it comes to me, they never ask if I want a meal, whether it's for dine-in or takeout, or discuss the price. They only give a cold reply if I ask them directly. Today, I tried not saying anything and waiting for them to ask me, but they just filled the meal with rice and tookout without mentioning the price. I had to awkwardly pull out my 100-yuan bill to pay. Finally, the cashier just tossed the bento box onto the table. I don't understand why I get treated like this just for a meal. I can't even eat a single bite of that 75-yuan bento box.
review_3: The kind owner noticed we were from out of town and came over to explain where to park. Every dish in the bento box was delicious; considering current prices, the owner is incredibly generous! ♥️🧡❤️ Definitely a shop that caters to students.
review_4: The owner was very polite and even gave us a banana. The vegetable soup was delicious.
review_5: I happened to go there because today was a vegetarian day. It looked quite empty at first, but the food was delicious. A large box was only 85 yuan, and it suited my taste perfectly.

mandarin_name: 楽坡BonBox - 台北政大店 / 樂坡舒肥健康餐盒
address: No. 18, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, 116
opening_hours: Monday: 10:30 AM–1:30 PM, 4–7:30 PM | Tuesday: 10:30 AM–1:30 PM, 4–7:30 PM | Wednesday: 10:30 AM–1:30 PM, 4–7:30 PM | Thursday: 10:30 AM–1:30 PM, 4–7:30 PM | Friday: 10:30 AM–1:30 PM, 4–7:30 PM | Saturday: 10:30 AM–1:30 PM | Sunday: closed
review_1:
The chicken was delicious 🐤

The vegetables and starches were also just the right amount 🥬🍚🍱

It's a pretty easy-to-eat meal among healthy options

The seasoning is light 🧂

Perfect for customers who prefer lighter flavors.
review_2: I've bought vegetarian healthy meals from many places, and this one feels the best in terms of both portion size and vegetable combination!
review_3:
When I picked up my lunchbox today, the lovely owner told me I'd lost weight. I told her it was because of the Lebo (a type of yogurt drink), and she was overjoyed, saying she wanted to put my before/after photos on the counter as an advertisement! 🤣🤣

As someone who's switched to a lighter diet, I've lost 7 kg in the past six months thanks to regularly ordering Lebo and sticking to a sugar-free diet. 😂 The vegetables are juicy and refreshing, and I can change the meat according to how oily I want each day (if I want something oilier, I order chicken thigh or Matsusaka pork; if I'm tired of oil-free chicken breast, I can switch to crispy salt and pepper squid). This light flavor has almost become my baseline for taste (which is a bit dangerous in a world where strong flavors are all the rage 🥹).

Ordering Lebo on my phone while writing my thesis is actually quite therapeutic (like setting an alarm clock that goes off when it's time). My thoughts aren't overwhelmed by the strong seasonings, my stomach doesn't feel greasy afterward, and I don't feel uncomfortable even after sitting for a long time.

Anyway, thank you, NCCU Lebo! 🫶
review_4:
Current top-rated healthy meal

- Excellent separation of dry and wet ingredients
The purple rice is cooked to the perfect consistency, not mushy at all.
The only minor drawback is that the chicken has a strong chicken flavor, making it less delicious.
The squid is good. The basa fish is also good.
Hopefully, Line Pay will be available in the future.
review_5: Super convenient ordering and pickup! 🩷✨️ Plus, there are discounts!! The bento box comes with purple rice and lots of vegetables – perfect for getting your nutrition! I've ordered so many times since the start of the semester.

review_1: This chicken crepe is super delicious, 35 yuan, and very crispy.
