review_5:
1. The food preparation staff member was coughing violently. Although they were wearing a mask, can a mask completely prevent the virus? Are they trying to make the customers catch a cold too? They should probably replace them temporarily. We've never seen a breakfast shop staff member coughing violently and still continue preparing/packaging food.

2. There was an eggshell inside the omelet; it wasn't cleaned properly.

address: No. 52, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wanxing Village, Wenshan District, Taipei City, 116
opening_hours: Monday: 11:30 AM–11 PM | Tuesday: 11:30 AM–11 PM | Wednesday: 11:30 AM–11 PM| Thursday: 11:30 AM–11 PM | Friday: 11:30 AM–11 PM | Saturday: 11:30 AM–11 PM | Sunday: 11:30 AM–11 PM
review_1: A dessert shop all year round, offering sweet and delicious treats! A great place to chat and relax at affordable prices! The peanuts and lotus seeds are cooked thoroughly until smooth and creamy! A dessert after a meal is pure bliss!
review_2:
17.5 yuan for a single glutinous rice ball isn't exactly cheap.
The lid already had some black spots when I received it; I initially thought they were osmanthus seeds.
But when I opened it, I was shocked—the whole bowl of soup was black!
It wasn't just a tiny broken piece. Can a dish be served like this? (Photo of the glutinous rice ball itself; it looks completely uneaten.)

Every time I run into this auntie, nothing good ever comes of it.
I won't buy from her next time.
review_3:
The business is run by young people, and I don't even know if the soybeans used for their tofu pudding are genetically modified or not.

As someone who is also young or younger (I'm 19), I don't quite approve. Not only is the price higher than at the City Hall MRT station, but it also doesn't quite satisfy the palates of tofu pudding lovers. However, overall it's not bad (the tofu pudding has a very soft texture, which is nice for those who like soft textures).

Also, listening to fast-paced Korean pop music while eating tofu pudding is not recommended. One star is already a high score for the atmosphere.
review_4: The order was placed two days ago, and I called to confirm the items on the same day, but the delivery was completely late. If you can't accept a delivery, don't accept it. Small orders aren't important. This shows a lack of business integrity. What a terrible Lantern Festival!
review_5: Mixed shaved ice with four toppings: condensed milk, aiyu jelly, rice cake, and Job's tears. Cash only.

address: 2F, No. 56, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wanxing Village, Wenshan District, Taipei City, 116
opening_hours: Monday: 11 AM–8 PM | Tuesday: 11 AM–8 PM | Wednesday: 11 AM–8 PM| Thursday: 11 AM–8 PM | Friday: 11 AM–8 PM | Saturday: 11 AM–8 PM | Sunday: 11 AM–8 PM
review_1:
One of the oldest and most enduring cafes at National Chengchi University, hidden on the second floor is a small café with limited seating but a comfortable atmosphere. Minimum charge is one drink.

The popular waffles are made to order and take about 10 minutes, perfect for sharing between two or more people.

The salads are also generously portioned and made with high-quality ingredients, offering excellent value for money.
review_2:
Apt. Café is a café located on the second floor of No. 56, Section 2, Zhinan Road, Wenshan District, Taipei City. Near National Chengchi University, it's popular with students and hipsters. This café is known for its retro style, pet-friendly environment, and comfortable atmosphere.

⸻
🏠 Environment and Atmosphere

Apt. Café is tucked away on the second floor of an old apartment building. A small model at the entrance guides customers upstairs. The interior features a collage-style retro design, with various old chairs and wall decorations creating a nostalgic and comfortable atmosphere. The café has approximately 36 to 42 seats, suitable for reading, working, dating, or gatherings with friends. In addition, the café is home to several cats, such as Black & White, Taro Ball, and Tieguanyin, providing customers with therapeutic companionship. ￼ ￼ ￼

⸻

☕ Food and Drinks

The small apartment offers a diverse range of food and drinks at affordable prices, ranging from approximately NT$60 to NT$200. Among the food, the curry chicken pita bread and French duck breast crepe are particularly popular; the former is generously filled and refreshingly flavorful, while the latter is served with a salad and mashed potatoes, offering a rich texture. Desserts include a blueberry tart, where the blueberries are fresh, but the crust is a bit soggy. For drinks, there are various options such as hot Earl Grey milk tea and grapefruit bubble tea, but some customers have commented that some drinks are watery or overly sweet.

⸻

🐾 Pet-Friendly and Facilities

The small apartment is a pet-friendly cafe where cats roam freely, creating a warm atmosphere. Free Wi-Fi and power outlets are also provided, making it suitable for extended periods of work or reading on a laptop. There are no time limits on weekdays, but a 3-hour limit applies on weekends. ￼ ￼ ￼
review_3: The apartment recently upgraded to premium coffee beans and a new coffee machine, and the coffee it brews is now smooth, not bitter, and has a rich, delicious flavor. We ordered Indian curry beef stew, spicy cheese beef roti 🍕, and honey lemon pancakes 🧇, all of which were very tasty. There are cute cats walking around, the interior lighting is beautiful, the atmosphere is great, and it has the warmth of home. It's a great place for afternoon tea and highly recommended.
review_4: This small apartment is very busy and the business is very good. I ordered a latte, which didn't taste like coffee at all. Maybe they added too much milk. I'm just guessing. My friend said this American coffee is on par with those in convenience stores. That's it.
review_5:
A cat cafe near National Chengchi University (on the second floor).
The food is good, and the lighting creates a great atmosphere.
Wi-Fi is available, and there's a minimum spend on one drink.
The lemon tart and chamomile tea are both good. There are many laid-back cats inside. It's suitable for meetings, casual conversations, or relaxing.

address: No. 91, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, 文山區 Wenshan District, Taipei City, 116
opening_hours: Monday: 8 AM–2 PM, 5:30–9:30 PM | Tuesday: 8 AM–2 PM, 5:30–9:30 PM | Wednesday: 8 AM–2 PM, 5:30–9:30 PM| Thursday: 8 AM–2 PM, 5:30–9:30 PM | Friday: 8AM–2 PM | Saturday: 8AM–2:30PM | Sunday: closed
review_1: The Thick Beef Burger at the same price point isn't as good as Li Bai's Thick Beef and Egg Pancake with Flower Sauce. That one truly deserves the name "Thick Beef." The almond milk seems to have been diluted with water; it's quite thin and weak.
review_2: The breakfast items are delicious, especially the fried milk balls (5 for NT$50), which are even better than cheese sticks! They're incredibly cheesy and stretchy! The restaurant is often packed with students during mealtimes. The only downside is that the woodwork next to the seats is a bit dusty, as it's close to customers' heads. I recommend wiping it down regularly, perhaps weekly.
review_3:
Pan-fried chicken thigh with whole wheat toast and an egg. The chicken thigh was a large piece, very satisfying, and perfectly seasoned. Served with cucumber and other vegetables, it made for a very balanced meal!

Paired with unsweetened soy milk, it was the perfect start to the day!

Ordering by scanning a QR code is very convenient, and the air conditioning is very comfortable in the summer.

Affordable price, highly recommended!
review_4:
I can't allow my favorite restaurant to only get 4 stars!

It's always packed, but the staff are always so friendly. I highly recommend the roast pork and cheese sandwich, the owner's favorite egg pancake (with chili), and the double-flavor thick slice (Oreo milk crisp + golden sand milk crisp). The black tea soy milk is also incredibly delicious—don't hesitate to order the XL size!!

The food is delicious and the prices are reasonable; it's unbeatable around National Chengchi University!
review_5: Located opposite the main gate of National Chengchi University, the restaurant is clean and bright, and very popular. The portions are generous, and the prices are reasonable, making it good value for money. They use high-quality ingredients. Also, I highly recommend the soy milk tea! It's inexpensive and delicious!

mandarin_name: 雞肉本家 政大店 (雞肉飯專賣店/健康餐)
english_name: Chicken Benjia: Taiwanese Chicken Rice & Healthy Bento (NCCU)
address: No. 81, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wanxing Village, Wenshan District, Taipei City, 116
opening_hours: Monday: 11 AM–8:30 PM | Tuesday: 11 AM–8:30 PM | Wednesday: 11 AM–8:30 PM| Thursday: 11 AM–8:30 PM | Friday: 11 AM–8:30 PM | Saturday: 11 AM–8:30 PM | Sunday: 11 AM–8:30 PM
review_1: This recently opened restaurant offers fast service and food preparation. Considering the price-performance ratio, it's a good option near the school. I recommend the curry chicken; their chicken quality is good, and the curry is quite delicious. As for the lion's head meatballs, I personally found them a bit oily, and the flavor wasn't as strong as I expected, so it's a matter of personal preference. I personally would prefer a stronger flavor but less oily. From a price point perspective, the most recommended option is actually the basic chicken rice with curry—a practical choice. However, if you want double chicken or double lion's head meatballs, consider that carefully.
review_2: First time eating at Chicken Benjia, and it was surprisingly delicious! The chicken thigh meat was tender and not greasy at all. The chili was very fragrant, and the addition of fried shallots made me, who doesn't usually eat much rice, have an extra bowl. I recommend it to those who prefer a lighter taste; you don't need to add soy sauce. The handsome manager provided excellent service 👍 His eyes are captivating 🤣
review_3: The chicken was quite tender, but unfortunately there was no scallion oil or chili dipping sauce, and the sauce was a bit sparse, making the rice a little dry. The curry sauce was alright.
review_4:
After hearing about this legendary Chicken Honka restaurant from my family, I decided to give it a try. This time, I ordered their signature Double Chicken Rice, and the delicate aroma hit me as soon as it arrived. The chicken was tender and well-seasoned, with just the right balance of salty and sweet, making it a delicious pairing with rice. However, towards the end, a slight fishy smell was noticeable, which slightly detracted from the dish.

Also, despite the "double" portion, the portion wasn't as generous as I'd expected, which I think is a bit high for the price. Overall, Chicken Honka is a great place to try something new. The tender texture of the chicken is indeed unique, but the price is a bit high, and the fishy smell towards the end makes me hesitate to give it a good review. Perhaps I'll try other dishes next time to see if I experience a different experience.
review_5:
I stopped by a large crowd to try it out.

The waitress was very friendly. Since the dining area was full, she suggested I order first and then give her my pick-up number when a table became available.

You find your own table and tell the staff to prepare your food. They'll call your number when it's ready, and you can pick it up yourself.

I ordered "Lion's Head Chicken Rice" and "Delicious Chicken Rice." The portion size might be a bit small for a guy, but just right for a girl. Adding chili and scallion sauce (a must-have if you don't mind) really enhanced the meal. The side of boiled cabbage was a nice touch (I personally love it), the triangular tofu was very flavorful, and the chicken was tender and delicious—a thoughtful touch, especially since it was boneless.

The lion's head meatballs were also very flavorful and went well with rice.

The price is on the higher side; students might feel it's not good value for money. However, it's definitely worth trying.

english_name: Grass Jelly & Brown Sugar Shaved Ice
address: No. 79, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wanxing Village, Wenshan District, Taipei City, 116
opening_hours: Monday: 2PM–11PM | Tuesday:  2PM–11PM  | Wednesday:  2PM–11PM | Thursday:  2PM–11PM  | Friday:  2PM–11PM  | Saturday:  2PM–11PM  | Sunday:  closed
review_1: This is the first time I've had red bean soup that wasn't too sweet, yet it was incredibly delicious. The taro topping was smooth and creamy, and the taro balls were soft and chewy. It's truly the perfect choice for warming your heart and stomach in winter! 👍
review_2: A cold winter day, a warm bowl of grass jelly, warms the stomach and warms the heart.
review_3: I ordered grass jelly with mung bean soup. It's been so long since I've had such a simple, refreshing, and guilt-free dessert!
review_4: It's super delicious, the ingredients are generous, the shaved ice itself has flavor, and it's cheap and big.
review_5:
The owner is generous with the ingredients! I love her! It feels so nice to eat here after practice or after class!

Wishing her booming business and the owner good health and safety (I definitely want to come back for this after I graduate! 😋)

mandarin_name: 摩斯漢堡 政大指南店
address: No. 71, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wanxing Village, Wenshan District, Taipei City, 116
opening_hours: Monday: 6 AM–11 PM | Tuesday:  6 AM–11 PM  | Wednesday: 6 AM–11 PM | Thursday:  6 AM–11 PM  | Friday:  6 AM–11 PM  | Saturday:  6 AM–11 PM | Sunday:  6 AM–11 PM
review_1: MOS Burger is the closest burger to the main gate of National Chengchi University. It's conveniently located, bright and clean, with delicious food 😋, a quiet space where you can talk softly, and a comfortable and relaxing ambiance. You can even enjoy the view from the second-floor windows. There's a handwashing station at the recycling area, which is quite nice.
review_2: Both the male and female staff were very friendly, and the food quality at this restaurant has always been excellent 👍. Currently, there are rewards for using EasyPay, so I can definitely keep eating here.
review_3: The quietest MOS Burger I've ever been to... and it didn't even smell of fried food!
review_4: Takeout: Fresh Green Roasted Chicken Cheeseburger $80, Fresh Mushroom Soup $55. ...Checking Gemini's recommended low-sugar meals, the roasted chicken will have to wait a bit.
review_5: A rare find! It's the perfect place to eat and relax near National Chengchi University. The environment is clean, and there are even power outlets in the seating area! The staff are also very friendly. 😘

address: Ln 77 ZhiNan Rd sec 2, Wanxing Village, Wenshan District, Taipei City, 116
opening_hours: Monday: 4–11 PM | Tuesday:  4–11 PM  | Wednesday: 4–11 PM | Thursday:  4–11 PM  | Friday:  4–11 PM  | Saturday:  4–11 PM | Sunday: closed
review_1: The fried rice at Ruins is incredibly well-made, with distinct grains, a nice aroma, and a fragrant, non-greasy texture. I absolutely love it! I called to ask if they were open, and the owner was patient, polite, and easy to communicate with. Every time I visit the zoo, I make sure to come here to eat my cravings. 🤍 The fried rice I had today was a little bland, but the aroma was still as fragrant as ever. The environment is quite clean for a food stall; the tabletops and floors aren't dirty or sticky. The prices are excellent, and the stir-fried vegetables are delicious, too, for only 60 yuan! I'll definitely be back next time. 🤍
review_2: Fast service, delicious taste, and low price!
review_3:
It's crowded on weekday evenings.

I passed by and saw a lot of people, so I decided to try it. The prices are average, considered cheap for Taipei.

Because it's crowded, the food takes a while to arrive. The pork fried rice is okay, not too salty. The satay beef is very salty; you definitely need to eat it with rice. The pork belly is also on the salty side and the texture is just average. The omelet and shrimp omelet are average. The meatball soup is average.

Overall, the dining experience is average, but it's cheap. Most of the diners are university students.
review_4: One of the dinner options for students at National Chengchi University, it's located right outside the McDonald's. The restaurant has a semi-open space, so it might get quite hot in the summer. Today I ordered the lamb fried rice. The seasoning was average, the rice grains were fairly separate but a bit dry, and there was a generous amount of greens. They seemed to have been added just before serving and were undercooked, still tasting raw. The lamb was too tough and chewy. Adding rice costs an extra NT$20. Next time I'd like to try the oyster omelet; I think it's quite good! Additional note: The best part is that adding a poached egg only costs NT$10 – a truly generous restaurant.
review_5: One of the few restaurants near National Chengchi University that stays open relatively late. Beef fried rice is NT$90, stir-fried cabbage is NT$60, and a poached egg is NT$10. The beef fried rice is quite fluffy and not soggy, but it still looks rather oily on the plate. If you order spicy, they'll add chili peppers while stir-frying. The poached egg is half-cooked with soy sauce paste and is very cheap.

english_name: Come See Pizza National Chengchi University
address: No. 55, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wanxing Village, Wenshan District, Taipei City, 116
opening_hours: Monday: 11:30 AM–8:30 PM | Tuesday:  11:30 AM–8:30 PM  | Wednesday: 11:30 AM–8:30 PM | Thursday:  11:30 AM–8:30 PM| Friday:  11:30 AM–8:30 PM  | Saturday:  11:30 AM–8:30 PM| Sunday: 11:30 AM–8:30 PM
review_1: Come See Pizza, the cheese-stirring pizza chain at National Chengchi University, has actually been open for a while. I'd only been there once with a tour group, and I always thought it only offered takeout. I was surprised to learn they also have a dine-in area. This time, I had the chance to dine in with friends and enjoy freshly baked pizzas, a completely different experience from takeout. The pizzas arrived piping hot and fragrant, and each bite revealed their crispy texture and satisfying cheese-stirring cheese pull. The pizzas are quite generously sized; each slice is larger than those sold in stores, and two slices are usually enough to feel full. The flavors change regularly, depending on what's being made, so there's always a different surprise each time you visit. Overall, Come See Pizza isn't just for takeout; the dine-in experience is definitely worth trying.
review_2: The pizzas are reasonably priced and large, with a decent taste. They're perfect for when you want a pizza anytime. The downside is that the staff aren't particularly friendly or helpful.
review_3: A decent American-style pizza place. During my mealtime, I saw many international students and faculty dining there; the atmosphere was relaxed and casual. The pizzas were pre-baked and kept warm under a heat lamp. The Mexican beef pizza had Jalapeño peppers, but it wasn't very spicy. The chowder pizza really tasted like solid chowder; it got a bit greasy towards the end. The fried food was freshly fried and crispy, but it seemed to be frozen. A pizza, fried food, and drink combo meal cost around 140-150 yuan. Overall, it's a budget-friendly restaurant in a food court located on the outskirts of the university, clearly targeting students as its main clientele.
review_4:
I highly recommend the wild mushroom and spinach topping! Even my meat-loving dad loves it!

The crust is very chewy. At $399, it's great value for money! 👍
review_5: The pizza crust is chewy, which is what I love. I ordered two filling flavors that others liked but I didn't. I would like to try other flavors next time. The onion soup was not bad, but I wish there were other soup options.

address: No. 103, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wanxing Village, Wenshan District, Taipei City, 116
opening_hours: Monday: 11 AM–9 PM | Tuesday:  11 AM–9 PM  | Wednesday: 11 AM–9 PM | Thursday: 11 AM–9 PM | Friday: 11 AM–9 PM  | Saturday:  11 AM–9 PM| Sunday: 11 AM–9 PM
review_1: During the Spring Festival, there was a significant shortage of staff both inside and outside the dining area. After ordering, it took 40 minutes for the first dish to arrive, and another 50 minutes for the other three dishes to be served. The pork loin was cold, clearly indicating it had been fried for at least ten minutes without being handled. The rice was also somewhat cold, suggesting it had been served long before being processed. We understand the staff shortage during the Spring Festival, but if even basic food quality cannot be maintained, it's advisable to refrain from operating, lest it damage the reputation of the Fushengting chain.
review_2: Fushengting National Chengchi University branch has been open for almost two years. The restaurant has two floors of dining space, with a spacious and bright environment. On weekdays, it's mostly frequented by students, but today, taking advantage of the less crowded atmosphere, the overall ambiance was quite comfortable. The restaurant offers unlimited self-service rice, miso soup, and barley tea. If you choose a set meal, you also get free-flowing drinks, which is quite a good deal for those with larger appetites. The menu mainly features fried pork cutlets, such as pork loin and pork tenderloin, which are signature dishes. Their crispy exterior and tender interior are loved by many students. A unique option is the salt-grilled mackerel, a good choice for those who enjoy refreshing fish dishes with a slightly salty flavor. Overall, the dining environment is comfortable, and replenishing rice and hot soup is very convenient, making it a great place for casual gatherings or quick meals.
review_3: 😱! The pork wasn't cooked! I ordered from a food delivery platform, and it took over 40 minutes to walk there. That's bad enough, but the miso soup bowl was so small it barely filled halfway. Did your restaurant put gold in the soup? And the most outrageous thing is the pork wasn't even cooked?! I wonder if I ate something undercooked and got parasites...
review_4: The male clerk wearing glasses had terrible service on Sunday afternoon! He had a sour face the whole time, was completely impatient when taking orders or asking questions, and spoke in a sharp tone, as if customers owed him money. If this is the kind of work attitude, I really suggest he go home and rest; there's no need to vent his emotions on customers. I was in a good mood, but having to put up with someone's attitude while shopping is unbearable. I will definitely not go back.
review_5: The chicken strips I've had a few times before were tiny, barely the size of a finger, and the staff chatted louder than the customers. Today, the portion was huge, the environment was quiet, and the staff were very polite.

address: No. 57, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wanxing Village, Wenshan District, Taipei City, 116
review_1: I had heard about this place from a friend before, and this time I was lucky enough to have her bring us here to dine. Every dish was delicious, the portions were generous, the place was very popular, and it was great value for money. Highly recommended!
review_2:
Date of Visit: October 15, 2025
Very affordable prices, cheap and generous portions. A la carte dishes also have very generous portions.
Food comes out quickly, no long wait.
The overall taste is a little salty, but still palatable and goes well with rice.
Fried rice is highly recommended, suitable for a satisfying meal for students.
The dining environment is a bit cramped, and the cleanliness is average.
It's slightly unsuitable for lunchtime in summer because the air conditioning and fans aren't strong enough, making it a bit stuffy. Hot tea is provided to cut through the saltiness.
review_3:
The food is delicious and inexpensive, I'll definitely come again!

- Pork rib fried rice is their signature dish, the pork ribs are really good!

- Scallion beef rice bowl is cheap, delicious, and a large portion!

- Beef rice noodles have plenty of toppings, and the rice noodles are chewy and smooth.

- The scallion egg is huge!

- The stir-fried water spinach is very tasty!

- Tofu and vegetable soup is cheap, a large portion, and delicious!
review_4: The pork ribs were quite fragrant, the fried rice was good but a bit oily, and the price was cheap. If you happen to be passing by, you can give it a try.
review_5: The old restaurant near National Chengchi University truly lives up to its reputation; the oyster eggs are incredibly fresh, the tofu with shredded pork and dried bean curd is generously portioned, and the stir-fried beef with scallions is very flavorful. The staff are very friendly; when I asked to change the oyster eggs because I was worried they were busy, they were very helpful.

mandarin_name: 八方雲集鍋貼水餃專賣店 政大店
address: No. 47, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wanxing Village, Wenshan District, Taipei City, 116
opening_hours: Monday: 11 AM–7:30 PM | Tuesday:  11 AM–7:30 PM | Wednesday: 11 AM–7:30 PM | Thursday: 11 AM–7:30 PM | Friday: 11 AM–7:30 PM  | Saturday: 11 AM–7:30 PM | Sunday: 11 AM–7:30 PM
review_1: You can pay with LINE Pay or JKO Pay. They issue traditional invoices and have a queuing system, but I didn't see a display screen. If you're temporarily leaving, you'll probably have to ask a staff member to find out if your number has been called. The staff were friendly, and the business was booming!
review_2: The items on the paper menu did not match the actual items sold. I was rude when I politely inquired. I was clearly queuing according to the rules. I don't know why the store manager and staff were rude. They were in a hurry to let the people who squeezed in behind them go first. It had only been less than a minute. What was the rush? The service was terrible.
review_3: The staff are friendly and kind, especially the fat guy at the frying pan.
review_4: Dumplings 🥟, sesame noodles, hot and sour soup, and corn soup are all good, and they accept Line Pay, which is very convenient.
review_5: The beef in the beef noodle soup was too tough to chew. It was my first time ordering okra, and it was also quite old. The overall wait time was longer than for the potstickers, about 15 minutes. But it really wasn't worth it. I'd recommend ordering potstickers or sesame sauce noodles instead.

address: No. 1, Section 1, Xinguang Rd, Wanxing Village, Wenshan District, Taipei City, 116
opening_hours: Monday: 2 PM–12 AM | Tuesday: 2 PM–12 AM | Wednesday: 2 PM–12 AM | Thursday: 2 PM–12 AM | Friday: 2 PM–2 AM  | Saturday: 2 PM–2 AM | Sunday: 2 PM–12 AM
review_1: ☕️A cafe near National Chengchi University that also offers hookah service. ⚠️Two-hour time limit when full, no time limit when not full. 🛜Wi-Fi available. 🔌Some seats have power outlets (mainly wall-mounted seats). 💰Currently accepted payment methods: cash, bank transfer, Line Pay, and Jiekou (a payment platform). Pay in advance. ✅Electronic invoices are available and can be stored. 🕛Open until midnight. There are very few cafes near National Chengchi University that stay open late (as far as I know, there are only two), and this one being open until midnight is undoubtedly a boon for students who work or study late. The drinks are a bit pricey. For example, the extra-strong milk tea I ordered, which was only the size shown in the photo, cost NT$180 (September 2024 price). However, since there's no time limit when not full, it's still within an acceptable range, and the milk tea is quite good. The main attraction inside is the polar bear; there's also a cute large polar bear plush toy and an adorable polar bear in the aquarium. Regarding the ambiance, the shop is divided into two areas. One is the area near the entrance, which is the café; the other, accessible through a small curtain, is the hookah area. The café has three bar stools and about five two-person tables, while the hookah area has three sets of four-person tables. Strictly speaking, the seating capacity isn't large, but it wasn't full that day (the hookah area was full). It might be more crowded during exam season or on weekends. The café area has a nice atmosphere, but the hookah area is a bit more complicated. The hookah smell wafts to the front (you can actually smell a slight hookah smell as soon as you open the café door), and because the restrooms are at the back, you have to walk through the hookah area, so everyone who drinks hookah will inevitably smell it. Of course, hookah is similar to e-cigarettes, and the smell isn't as unpleasant as traditional cigarettes, but it's still secondhand smoke, so whether you can tolerate it is a matter of personal preference. In addition, the hookah area is very chill (and there's a TV), but correspondingly, the conversations are also quite loud, and you can hear the conversations from the hookah area from the coffee shop area as well.
review_2:
The drinks and desserts were both excellent, perfect for a gathering with friends.
The crepe cake wasn't too sweet, and the pancake filling had an ice cream-like texture.
review_3:
A very comfortable shop, and the owner is young and talkative. There aren't many seats, and it gets quite full on weekend afternoons. It seems there's room for adjustment in seating.

As for drinks, the tea is very good, but the mocha is just average.

If you're in the area, this is a worthwhile little shop to relax in.
review_4:
The staff were very polite.

However, after ordering, I waited for half an hour, and when I looked back, I found the staff were making drinks for another table, who had only just sat down... Looking around, all the other tables had shisha and drinks on them, and the staff were very friendly, attending to other customers. I was the only one left standing there, which was a pretty bad experience.
review_5: Although I'm not a shisha consumer, this is my favourite underrated gem for local working spaces. As long as it's not busy, it's a fantastic, quiet space to plug in and enjoy a treat while you study or get paperwork finished up. My only complaint would be that the hours aren't super consistent, so you kind of have to send a scout ahead to see if it's actually open when it says it will be. For patrons coming later in the evening though, this should be less of a problem.

address: No. 7, Lane 45, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan 116
cuisine_type: taiwanese, spinach cold noodles, noodle shop, asian, chinese
opening_hours: Monday: 11:00 AM-2:00 PM, 5:00 PM-8:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM-2:00 PM, 5:00 PM-8:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM-2:00 PM, 5:00 PM-8:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM-2:00 PM, 5:00 PM-8:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM-2:00 PM, 5:00 PM-8:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM-2:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
review_1: The signature Shredded Chicken Cold Noodles are fantastic! The noodles are made with fresh spinach juice, giving them a unique green color and a great chewy texture.
review_2: This is a great budget-friendly spot for students. The portions are decent for the price, and the free sweet black tea is a nice bonus on a hot day.
review_3: I highly recommend their side dishes, especially the pickled bean sprouts. They have a refreshing sour taste that is very appetizing and pairs perfectly with the noodles.
review_4: The beef jerky noodles are delicious and have a chewy texture. Although the portion of beef is a bit small, the overall flavor and the unique green noodles make it worth it.
review_5: The shop is a popular choice for NCCU students due to its affordable prices. It's often packed during peak hours, so be prepared to share a table in a simple dining environment.

english_name: Yongkang Street Left-Handed (Yong Kang Jie Zuo Pie Zi)
address: No. 10, Lane 119, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan 116
cuisine_type: Taiwanese stir-fry and rice dishes, asian, chinese
opening_hours: Monday: 11:30 AM-2:00 PM, 5:00 PM-8:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:30 AM-2:00 PM, 5:00 PM-8:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:30 AM-2:00 PM, 5:00 PM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:00 PM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:00 PM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed
review_1: The fried rice here is legendary, full of breath of the wok (wok hei) and very fragrant. Although they shifted to bento boxes recently due to the owner's injury, the quality remains great. Highly recommended for students looking for a filling meal.
review_2: The chicken cutlet rice is a must-try with crispy skin and juicy meat. The braised pork rice that comes with it is a delightful surprise. The dining area is clean and simple.
review_3: Very popular among NCCU students for its large portions and affordable prices. The beef fried rice and kung pao chicken are my favorites. The owner is very friendly and always greets guests warmly.
review_4: Even though they don't serve fried rice anymore, their bento options like teriyaki chicken are still top-notch. It's a great spot for a quick and satisfying lunch. The free drinks are a nice bonus.
review_5: Excellent value for money with most dishes under 130 NTD. The food is cooked to order and served piping hot. It's one of those hidden gems in the alleys that never disappoints.

mandarin_name: 梁社漢排骨 文山指南店

opening_hours: Monday: 10:30 AM – 8:30 PM | Tuesday: 10:30 AM – 8:30 PM | Wednesday: 10:30 AM – 8:30 PM | Thursday: 10:30 AM – 8:30 PM | Friday: 10:30 AM – 8:30 PM | Saturday: 10:30 AM – 8:30 PM | Sunday: 10:30 AM – 8:30 PM
review_1: The meal prep was a bit slow today, but it was acceptable given it was during the Lunar New Year period. The overall quality remains consistent.
review_2: The rose soy sauce chicken leg is quite tasty and has become one of my regular go-to choices. The flavor is well-balanced and the meat is tender.
review_3: The bento was still steaming hot when it was delivered, which made for a very pleasant dining experience. I appreciate the attention to food temperature.
review_4: The new packaging for the chicken leg rice looks much more refined and high-end than before. However, it's a bit of a pity that the miso soup now requires an extra fee.
review_5: The fried pork ribs are thick, juicy, and very satisfying. The side dishes are also quite fresh and not overly greasy compared to other bento shops.

address: No. 3, Lane 45, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan 116

opening_hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:00 PM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:00 PM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:00 PM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:00 PM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:00 PM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 11:30 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 11:30 AM – 9:00 PM
review_1: The soup base is made with natural ingredients and no artificial additives, making it very healthy and delicious. I especially like the Golden Kimchi Pork Pot. The meat quality is good and the broth is flavorful.
review_2: Great value for money! They offer unlimited braised pork rice, white rice, ice cream, and soda. The environment is clean and comfortable, perfect for a family gathering or a quick solo meal.
review_3: I highly recommend the Creamy Cheese & Milk Hot Pot. It's very rich and fragrant. The portions are generous, and the self-service bar is a huge plus. Definitely one of the best hot pot spots in the area.
review_4: The spicy Sichuan Peppercorn Hot Pot is amazing—numbing but not overly spicy. The staff is friendly, and the service is efficient even during busy hours. It's a great place to satisfy your hot pot cravings.
review_5: A cozy restaurant with a variety of soup bases to choose from. The braised pork rice is surprisingly good for a free side. It's affordable and filling, highly recommended for students and locals.

address: No. 1, Lane 45, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wanxing Village, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan 116
cuisine_type: taiwanese grilled meat bento, chinese, asian
opening_hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 4:30 PM – 8:30 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 4:30 PM – 8:30 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 4:30 PM – 8:30 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 4:30 PM – 8:30 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 4:30 PM – 8:30 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 4:30 PM – 8:30 PM | Sunday: Closed
review_1: The grilled meat is very flavorful and the portions are decent for the price. It's a great choice for a quick and satisfying lunch near the university.
review_2: I highly recommend the chicken leg bento; the meat is tender and the sauce is perfectly sweet and savory. It's my go-to place when I'm in a hurry.
review_3: A solid choice for students. The variety of meat options like pork belly and beef is great, though it can get quite busy during peak hours.
review_4: The portions are generous and the ingredients taste fresh. It's affordable and definitely one of the better bento spots in the NCCU area.
review_5: Delicious grilled rice boxes! The pork belly is perfectly charred and the seasoned egg is a must-add to any meal.

address: No. 10, Lane 45, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan 116
opening_hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 2:30 PM | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 2:30 PM | Thursday: 7:00 AM – 2:30 PM | Friday: 7:00 AM – 2:30 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 2:30 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 2:30 PM
review_1: The omelets are incredibly tender and flavorful. I especially loved the Mexican chicken omelet; it's a must-try here with its perfect balance of spices.
review_2: This place has a wonderful blue-and-white Greek-style decor that creates a great atmosphere. It's a nice spot for a high-quality breakfast near the university campus.
review_3: The portions are quite large and satisfying for the price. The blueberry bagel with fresh apple and cheese was very refreshing and a unique flavor combination.
review_4: The food is a bit more expensive than your typical traditional breakfast joint, but the quality of ingredients makes it worth it. Their grilled vegetable toast is a fantastic option.
review_5: The beef burgers are juicy and the sauces are well-balanced. It's a solid choice for a relaxing brunch if you are in the Wenshan area.

address: No. 167, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan 116
cuisine_type: Yunnan cuisine, rice noodles, chinese, asian
opening_hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM
review_1: Very authentic Yunnan cuisine near National Chengchi University. It’s a classic spot where students go for celebrations, offering high-quality dishes.
review_2: You must try their signature Jiao Ma Chicken; the meat is crispy and pairs perfectly with the sauce and cabbage. The small pot rice noodles are also highly recommended.
review_3: They offer traditional Yunnan dishes like Baba silk and steam pot chicken. The cross-bridge noodles are served piping hot and the broth is very sweet and flavorful.
review_4: The stir-fried Baba silk has a great chewy texture and the spicy sauce is delicious. It's a popular choice for students since you can get a discount with a student ID.
review_5: The beef rice noodles are a great value with tender beef chunks and a refreshing soup base. It's a very filling and satisfying meal for the price.

address: No. 153, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan 116
opening_hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 10:30 PM | Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 10:30 PM | Thursday: 11:30 AM – 10:30 PM | Friday: 11:30 AM – 10:30 PM | Saturday: 11:30 AM – 10:30 PM | Sunday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM
review_1: This is a great value hot pot restaurant for students, with a minimum price starting around 249 NTD for a set. The veggie bar is unlimited and features a wide variety of fresh greens and even some rare options like malabar spinach. It's a perfect spot for those who want to eat healthy on a budget.
review_2: The garlic clam soup base is absolutely delicious and very rich—definitely a highlight of the meal. They also have a massive 'noodle wall' with many types of instant noodles to choose from, which is fun for diners. The overall vibe is bright and clean, making it a pleasant place to eat.
review_3: While the price is very attractive, the dining experience can be a bit smoky if you opt for the iron plate roast meat since there's no exhaust pipe. The meat quality is standard for this price range, but the unlimited access to the salad bar and drinks makes it worth it. I'd recommend sticking to the hot pot to avoid the smoke.
review_4: I was impressed by the side dishes like the braised pork rice and the spicy duck blood, which are both unlimited. The restaurant is quite popular, especially with NCCU students, so it's best to make a reservation beforehand to avoid waiting. It's a solid choice for a filling meal near the university.
review_5: Service can be a bit slow during peak hours as the staff seems quite busy, but they are generally helpful and polite. We had an issue with the soup refill taking too long, and they kindly offered us free clams as compensation. It's a high CP value spot that I would visit again for a casual dinner.

address: No. 13, Section 1, Xinguang Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan 116
opening_hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 7:30 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 7:30 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 7:30 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 7:30 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 7:30 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 7:30 PM | Sunday: Closed
review_1: The fried wontons are exceptionally crispy and the filling is well-seasoned. At such an affordable price, it’s a must-order side dish every time.
review_2: Their signature dough drops have a wonderful chewy texture that isn't too soft. The soup is light and refreshing, perfect for a simple lunch.
review_3: The pork chop rice is a solid choice. The pork chop is thick and has a good bite, and the rice with braised sauce is very comforting.
review_4: This is a classic 'no-name' shop near the university. It’s a favorite for students because the food is fresh, cheap, and consistently good.
review_5: The braised tofu is a hidden gem here. It’s very flavorful and pairs perfectly with any of their noodle or rice dishes.

address: No. 1, 1st Floor, Lane 45, Section 2, Zhinan Rd, Wenshan District, Taipei City, Taiwan 116
cuisine_type: italian pasta, japanese curry, and rice bowls
opening_hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:00 PM – 8:30 PM | Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:00 PM – 8:30 PM | Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:00 PM – 8:30 PM | Thursday: 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:00 PM – 8:30 PM | Friday: 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:00 PM – 8:30 PM | Saturday: 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:00 PM – 8:30 PM | Sunday: 11:30 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:00 PM – 8:30 PM
review_1: The food is quite affordable and they offer free black tea and soup. I especially love their baked dishes; for just an extra 10 NT$, you can upgrade to a cheesy gratin which is really worth it.
review_2: A cozy little spot near NCCU with a great variety of choices including pasta, curry, and rice bowls. The seating is a bit cramped and you might have to share a table during peak hours, but the food comes out fast.
review_3: The cream chicken pasta is one of my favorites here. The sauce is rich and creamy, and the chicken is tender, making it a very satisfying meal for a student budget.
review_4: Their Japanese-style fried shrimp curry is delicious and the portion is generous. It is a great place for a quick lunch or dinner between classes, though it gets very crowded at noon.
review_5: I really enjoy the warm and homey atmosphere of this small restaurant. The owner is friendly, and the free soup is surprisingly good, often featuring corn or other fresh ingredients.

address: 2F., No. 131, Sec. 2, Zhinan Rd., Wenshan Dist., Taipei City 116, Taiwan
opening_hours: Monday: 11:30 AM-2:00 PM, 4:30 PM-9PM | Tuesday: 11:30 AM-2:00 PM, 4:30 PM-9PM | Wednesday: 11:30 AM-2:00 PM, 4:30 PM-9PM | Thursday: 11:30 AM-2:00 PM, 4:30 PM-9PM| Friday: 11:30 AM-2:00 PM, 4:30 PM-9PM | Saturday: closed | Sunday: 11:30 AM-2:00 PM, 5PM-9PM
review_1:
Here's my meal schedule:

18:14 Seated

18:20-18:26 Waiting at the unattended counter to pay

18:57 My friend's food arrived

19:07 Spicy rice cakes arrived

19:10 My food arrived

—————— Putting aside the slow delivery and the constant notifications from the delivery platform, what shocked me most today was the bibimbap that arrived thirteen minutes later—the later bowl was practically porridge! If you asked me today what the softest thing I've ever eaten was, it would definitely be this bibimbap.

I've eaten liquid food when my father was critically ill, which shows how incredibly tolerant I am of food—even bulletproof pork chops and hairy pig's feet in the military. Given that, there are probably fewer than five times in my life I've thought this food was awful. But today, in 2026, Goguryeo has achieved that.

Speaking of the other bowl of bibimbap, I ate it in utter shock. I have to suspect it wasn't even cooked by the same person. Looking at that incredibly ordinary bowl of bibimbap, and then at mine, it's almost laughable.

I rarely find food truly awful, but my principle of restraint is not to waste food. Out of respect for food, I can still give it a one-star rating.
review_2: The owner is a very friendly and straightforward person, but because the shop is busy, she can be a bit rushed and disorganized, so please be patient 😂 The main course includes a side dish, rice is self-service, and vegetarian options are available! Overall, it's a good restaurant, I would definitely visit again 🙂‍↕️
review_3: A long-established Korean restaurant near National Chengchi University! Their army stew and bibimbap are delicious 😋 I've been eating there since I was a student. A set meal is only NT$35, which includes two side dishes and a drink 🥤
review_4: I went to this restaurant because I was attending classes at National Chengchi University. It was a big deal that it was open during the summer vacation, and the food was incredibly delicious. I ordered the custard tofu hotpot set meal. It comes with two side dishes and a drink of your choice. I wanted a mild spice option, but the custard hotpot wasn't spicy enough. The mild spice was just right, not too spicy, and I even finished the soup. It was absolutely delicious.
review_5: I went to the dentist next door and then went upstairs for dinner. The beef bibimbap I ordered today was delicious 😋. The set meal (which included two side dishes and a bottle of soda, with lemon black tea available for self-service) was also very good. The seafood pancake was crispy with a soft and chewy filling. The owner was very straightforward, and the dining environment was quite ordinary. You can order by QR code. You should give it a try!
